title: Add persistent history storage to backend and wire frontend to use it

requirements.text: Ensure the Motoko backend actor uses stable storage (stable var or StableHashMap) for all health records so that data persists across canister upgrades and page refreshes. The actor must expose: (1) an update function `addRecord` that accepts all risk predictor fields (age, BMI, blood pressure, glucose, hemoglobin, cholesterol, gender, smoker, computed risk score, risk level, and a timestamp) and stores the record, and (2) a query function `getAllRecords` that returns all stored records in reverse-chronological order.

quotes: Persistent history storage is the one piece still pending
saving risk predictions and X-ray results to the backend so they survive page refreshes

acceptanceCriteria: Health records are stored in stable variables so they survive canister upgrades.
`addRecord` accepts all required fields and persists the entry.
`getAllRecords` returns all persisted records sorted newest-first.
No data loss occurs after a simulated canister upgrade cycle.

requirements.text: Update the `useAddRecord` and `useGetAllRecords` React Query hooks in `frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts` to correctly map the backend's `addRecord` and `getAllRecords` Candid interfaces. Ensure the `HealthRecord` TypeScript type includes all fields returned by the backend (age, BMI, blood pressure, glucose, hemoglobin, cholesterol, gender, smoker, riskScore, riskLevel, timestamp).

quotes: saving risk predictions and X-ray results to the backend so they survive page refreshes

acceptanceCriteria: `useAddRecord` mutation calls the backend `addRecord` function with all required fields.
`useGetAllRecords` query correctly fetches and deserializes all records from the backend.
TypeScript `HealthRecord` type matches the Candid-generated type with all fields present.
Query cache is invalidated and records list refreshes after a new record is added.

requirements.text: Update `frontend/src/pages/RiskPredictor.tsx` to call `useAddRecord` on successful form submission, passing all form values plus the computed risk score and risk level, so every prediction is persisted to the backend.

quotes: saving risk predictions and X-ray results to the backend so they survive page refreshes

acceptanceCriteria: After submitting the risk predictor form, a record is saved to the backend.
The saved record includes all input fields plus the computed risk score and risk level.
A loading indicator is shown while the save is in progress.
An error message is shown if the save fails.

requirements.text: Update `frontend/src/pages/Dashboard.tsx` and `frontend/src/pages/History.tsx` to load records via `useGetAllRecords` from the backend instead of any local in-memory state. Both pages should display live persisted data that survives page refreshes.

quotes: saving risk predictions and X-ray results to the backend so they survive page refreshes

acceptanceCriteria: Dashboard table populates with records fetched from the backend on page load.
History page cards populate with records fetched from the backend on page load.
Data is still present after a full browser page refresh.
A loading skeleton or spinner is shown while records are being fetched.
An empty-state message is shown when no records exist yet.

constraints: Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui.
All Motoko logic must remain in backend/main.mo as a single actor.
Generate migration.mo only if the stable data schema changes from the existing backend.
nonGoals: Persisting X-ray analysis results to the backend (only risk predictor records are in scope).
Adding new pages or navigation items.
Changing the existing visual theme or layout.
Implementing Internet Identity authentication gating for record access.
